title: Dynamic versus static power components
description: Dynamic power scales with activity, capacitance, and voltage squared, while static power comes from transistor leakage.
body: Use when deciding where to focus power optimization. Do not treat all power as one number, since dynamic and static respond to different levers. Actions: separate the dynamic contribution from switching from the static leakage, identify which dominates the state of interest, and target the right technique. Validation signals include a power breakdown attributing each component and optimization moving the dominant term. Risk boundary: optimizing the wrong component wastes effort, so flag any plan that ignores the dominant power source for the design's operating state.
